Detective Elara Voss had seen her share of disappearances, but nothing like this. Three in a month—each one a woman, each one gone without a trace. The first had been a fisherman’s daughter, found weeks later in a tide pool, her face frozen in a silent scream. The second, a schoolteacher, vanished during a storm, her boat drifting empty into the night. The third, a librarian, left behind only a half-finished book and a faint scent of salt on her skin.

The townsfolk whispered of the old legend—the Wraith of Blackmoor, a spirit said to lure the curious into the sea. But Elara wasn’t one for ghosts. She preferred the tangible, the real. And yet, the deeper she dug, the more the town seemed to resist her. The constable avoided her questions. The fishermen refused to speak of the waters beyond the cove. Even the local innkeeper, a woman with eyes like storm clouds, refused to serve her a second cup of tea.

For years, the town had been a place of quiet charm, but now, a shadow had crept over it.
